structure.title: 4 Extended Link Services
structure.summary: Covers Extended Link Services (ELS) basics and request rules: an ELS request solicits a destination `Nx_Port` function; an ELS reply is required unless otherwise specified. Defines single-`Sequence` request/reply structure, `ELS_Command` code in the first word/first byte of `Payload`, one-`Exchange` request/reply pairing, `Last Sequence bit` termination on `LS_ACC`, and normal `Exchange`/`Sequence` management per `FC-FS-2`. Specifies frame fields: `TYPE` = `01h`, `R_CTL` values, `Payload`, `D_ID`, `S_ID`, `LFA`, valid addresses, well known addresses, and `FLOGI` before all ELSs in a Fabric. `Table 2 – Extended Link Services Routing Bits and Information Categories` defines `R_CTL` routing/info categories: `0001b` Solicited Data, `0010b` Request, `0011b` Reply, Others Reserved; note `CSU`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions, procedure/flow.

children.title: 4.2 Extended Link Service requests
children.summary: Section `4.2 Extended Link Service requests` defines when and how a Sequence Initiator sends an ELS Sequence to solicit a destination `Nx_Port` link-level function/service. Covers request preconditions and addressing rules: ELS requests shall not be issued before completion of `N_Port Login` unless otherwise noted; `FLOGI` is required before all ELSs if a Fabric is present; `Table 3` applies to ELSs sent to or received by all valid addresses, including well known addresses. Specifies `LFA` usage: destination ID (`D_ID`) for `LINIT` and `LSTS` ELS Request Sequences, source ID (`S_ID`) in Reply Sequences; no other Sequences shall be directed to `LFA`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Table `2 – Extended Link Services Routing Bits and Information Categories`: defines `R_CTL` routing/information values for ELS frames.

children.title: 4.2.24 Registered Link Incident Report (RLIR)
children.summary: Section 4.2.24 defines the Registered Link Incident Report (`RLIR`) `ELS`: how a reporting `Nx_Port` sends a `Link Incident Record` for an incident port to a registered `Nx_Port`, with roles/terms `incident port`, `connected port`, `connected node`, `reporting port`, `registered port`, and `reporting node`. Response/branch handling: normal reply is `LS_ACC` `ELS Sequence` with no Payload; unsupported `RLIR` returns `LS_RJT` with reason code `"Command not supported"`; unacceptable record type returns `LS_RJT` with `"Unable to perform command request"`. Procedure/flow: recognize incidents when an incident code is defined in `table 72`, generate one record per incident, send `RLIR ELSs` to registered recipients, then discard after all required sends. Completion/exception conditions: vendor-specific record capacity, discard oldest record on overflow, optional delay after `Link Failure`, report only if failure persists past FC-4 timeout. Tables/Figures: `table 72` (incident codes referenced for recognition); no figures.

children.title: 4.2.24.1 Description
children.summary: Defines `4.2.24 Registered Link Incident Report (RLIR)` purpose and terminology for link incident reporting: `incident port`, `connected port`, `connected node`, `reporting port`, `registered port`, `reporting node`. Core behavior: `RLIR ELS` lets a reporting `Nx_Port` send a `Link Incident Record` to a registered `Nx_Port`; normal response is `LS_ACC ELS Sequence` with no Payload. Response/branch handling: unsupported recipient returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Command not supported`; unsupported record type returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Unable to perform command request`. Mentions incident recognition via incident code in `table 72`; if a registered recipient exists, a `Link Incident Record` is created and reported. Exception/completion conditions: delayed reporting after `Link Failure`; some `FC-4` recovery may suppress reporting unless timeout persists beyond FC-4 specified period. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

children.title: 4.2.24.2 Link Incident reporting procedure
children.summary: Procedure/flow for `4.2.24.2 Link Incident reporting procedure`: how a reporting node and reporting port handle creation, retention, transmission, and discard of `Link Incident Record` objects after a link incident. Core rules: the incident port’s reporting node generates one `Link Incident Record` per link incident; record capacity is vendor specific; on resource exhaustion, the oldest `Link Incident Record` is discarded and the new record retained. The selected reporting port presents records by sending `RLIR ELSs` to ports registered as valid recipients of `Link Incident Records`; after sending all required `RLIR ELSs` containing the `Link Incident Record`, the port may discard the record. Content types: procedure/flow; completion/exception conditions (resource exhaustion, post-send discard). Tables: none. Figures: none.

children.title: 4.2.24.3 Protocol
children.summary: Section `4.2.24.3 Protocol` covers RLIR delivery protocol selection and the `RLIR Request Sequence` / `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence` for sending a `RLIR ELS` from reporting port to registered port. Includes procedure/flow, response/branch handling, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions, and message/frame format. Key rules: send first to ports registered to always receive `RLIR`; on failure, try ports registered to conditionally receive `RLIR`; `RLIR ELS` is successfully transmitted only if `LS_ACC` is received within `2xR_A_TOV`; registration may be discarded after unsuccessful delivery; vendor specific behavior if no registration exists. `S_ID` = reporting port, `D_ID` = registered port. Table 67 – RLIR Payload: defines `RLIR Payload` layout, including `RLIR (79h)`, `Common Link Incident Record Length`, `Common Link Incident-Format Descriptor Length`, `Common Link Incident Descriptor`, `IQ`, `IC`, `EPAI (domain/area of ISL)`, and `Specific Link Incident Record`.

children.title: 4.2.24.4 Request Sequence
children.summary: RLIR Request Sequence message/frame format for `RLIR` ELS payload and field semantics: addressing uses `S_ID` = reporting port, `D_ID` = registered port; payload starts `RLIR (79h)`. Covers `Link Incident Record Format`, `Common Link Incident Record Length`, `Common Link Incident Descriptor Length`, `Specific Link Incident Record Length`, `Common Link Incident Record`, `Common Link Incident Descriptor`, `Incident Qualifier (IQ)`, `Incident Code (IC)`, `Expansion Port address identifier (EPAI)`, `Transaction ID`, `Time Stamp Format`, `Time Stamp`; identifiers include `N_Port_Name`, `Node_Name`, `Port_ID`, `Fabric_Name`; values/codes include `00h`, `01h-FFh`, `4h`, `16`, `64`, `0`, `252`; states/conditions mention `Link-Failure`, `Offline State`, `OL3 State`, `LF1 State`, `R_T_TOV`. Tables: Table 67 – RLIR Payload (payload layout); Table 68 – Link Incident Record Format (format codes); Table 69 – Common Link Incident Record Data (record fields); Table 70 – Time Stamp Format values (timestamp encodings); Table 71 – Incident Qualifier / Continued (IQ bit meanings, severity/FRU/E_Port flags); Table 72 – Incident Code values (incident types). Content types: message/frame format, timer/negotiation, completion/exception conditions.

children.title: 4.2.24.5 Reply Sequence
children.summary: Section `4.2.24.5 Reply Sequence` covers RLIR reply handling: `LS_RJT` rejects the `RLIR Request`; `LS_ACC` accepts the `RLIR Request` and its `Link Incident Record`. Includes `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload`, which defines the `LS_ACC Payload` message/frame format: `Bits 31 .. 24`, `23 .. 16`, `15 .. 08`, `07 .. 00`; `Word 0 = 02h 00h 00h 00h`. Contained content types: response/branch handling, message/frame format, completion/exception conditions. Page text immediately continues into `4.2.25 Link Incident Record Registration (LIRR)`: `LIRR ELS` registration/de-registration procedure/flow, normal response `LS_ACC` with no Payload, exception `LS_RJT` with reason code `Command not supported` or `Unable to perform command request`, and destination identifier `Management Server (FFFFFAh)`.

children.title: 4.2.25 Link Incident Record Registration (LIRR)
children.summary: Section `4.2.25 Link Incident Record Registration (LIRR)` covers `LIRR ELS` registration/de-registration of an `Nx_Port` to receive `Registered Link Incident Report (RLIR) ELS`, including Fabric registration to `Management Server (FFFFFAh)`. Describes `4.2.25.1 Description` and `4.2.25.2 Registration for Link Incident Records`: when to send `LIRR ELS`, use of ports of interest, required determination of address identifier(s), and per-format recipient registration lists. Response/branch handling: normal response is `LS_ACC ELS Sequence` with no Payload; unsupported `LIRR ELS` returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Command not supported`; failures or unsupported format return `Unable to perform command request`; duplicate register/de-register and unregistered de-registration both accept with `LS_ACC` and no further action. Table `73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload` shows the `LS_ACC Payload` format/value `02h 00h 00h 00h`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions, message/frame format.

children.title: 4.2.25.1 Description
children.summary: 4.2.25.1 Description — Link Incident Record Registration (LIRR) ELS: core topic is registering or de-registering a source Nx_Port to receive Registered Link Incident Report (RLIR) ELS notifications, including Fabric registration via Management Server (`FFFFFAh`). Refers to 4.2.24.1 for terms. Normal response to a `LIRR ELS Sequence` is `LS_ACC ELS Sequence` with no Payload. If unsupported, recipient returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Command not supported`; if unable to perform registration function or format unsupported, returns `LS_RJT ELS Sequence` with reason code `Unable to perform command request`. Contains: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload: defines `LS_ACC Payload` format for `RLIR Request`; value shown is Word 0 = `02h 00h 00h 00h`.

children.title: 4.2.25.2 Registration for Link Incident Records
children.summary: Section `4.2.25.2 Registration for Link Incident Records` covers procedure/flow and response/branch handling for `LIRR ELS` registration/de-registration of an `Nx_Port` to receive `Link Incident Records` from `ports of interest`. A `ULP` registers after determining its address identifier and the address identifiers of ports of interest, indicating the registration format; recipient returns `LS_ACC ELS` with no Payload. De-registration also uses `LIRR ELS` with indicated format. Completion/exception conditions: de-registering a port not registered returns `LS_ACC ELS` and no further action; registering a port already registered for the specified format returns `LS_ACC ELS` and no further action. Recipient maintains separate established registration lists for each registration format. Includes `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload`, showing the `LS_ACC Payload` format with `Word 0 = 02h 00h 00h 00h` (message/frame format).

children.title: 4.2.25.3 Responsibilities of Valid-Registered Recipients
children.summary: Core topic: responsibilities of valid-registered recipients for Link Incident Records and mismatch handling. A valid-registered recipient shall receive/process Link Incident Records generated per Link Incident reporting procedure (`4.2.24.2`); ULP coordination is out of scope. If a valid registered recipient receives a Link Incident Record whose format does not match its registration format, the recipient port shall reject the `RLIR ELS` with reason code `Unable to perform command request`. Related surrounding conditions on this page: Logout between a node and a valid-registered-recipient node causes de-registration; full registration list causes rejection with `unable to perform command request`. Table present: `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload` — defines `RLIR LS_ACC Payload`. Named anchors: `RLIR ELS`, `Link Incident Records`, `Link Incident reporting procedure`, `Unable to perform command request`, `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload`. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

children.title: 4.2.25.4 Protocol
children.summary: 4.2.25.4 Protocol: defines the protocol outline for Link Incident Record registration, specifically `LIRR Request Sequence` and `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Core anchors and identifiers present in the provided text: `LIRR`, `RLIR`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `LIRR Payload`, `Link Incident Record Registration Function`, `Registration Format`, `7Ah`, `02h`, reason code `Unable to perform command request`. Related behavioral conditions referenced in adjacent protocol text: add/remove registration, de-registration on `Logout`, rejection when registration list is full, rejection for format mismatch. Table present: `Table 73 – RLIR LS_ACC Payload`, which defines/shows the `RLIR LS_ACC Payload` layout. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

children.title: 4.2.25.5 Request Sequence
children.summary: Covers `4.2.25.5 Request Sequence` for `LIRR` registration requests for Link Incident Records: addressing and payload definition for the request message. Content types: `procedure/flow`, `message/frame format`. `Addressing`: `S_ID` = source port requesting registration for Link Incident Records; `D_ID` = port receiving the registration request. `Payload`: `LIRR Payload` format shown in `Table 74 – LIRR Payload`, including `Word 0` = `LIRR (7Ah) 00h 00h 00h`, and `Word 1` fields `Link Incident Record-Registration Function`, `Reserved`, `Registration Format`. Field rule present: `Registration Function` specifies mode of registration, i.e., whether the recipient node adds or removes the sending `Nx_Port` to or from its list of registered recipients. No figures. No explicit response/branch handling, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions in this subsection.

children.title: 4.2.25.6 Reply Sequence
children.summary: Section `4.2.25.6 Reply Sequence` covers response/branch handling for `LIRR Request`: `LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the `LIRR Request`. Nearby context on the same page defines request parameters used by this exchange: field `Registration Function` in `Table 75 – Registration Function` (defines registration actions/codes `00h Reserved`, `01h Set registration – conditionally receive`, `02h Set registration – always receive`, `FFh Clear registration`, `03h - FEh Reserved`) and `Link Incident Record-Registration Format` / `Link Incident Record-Registration Type` in `Table 76 – Link Incident Record-Registration Format` (defines format codes `00h Common Format`, `01h - FFh Specific-coded value`). Content types present: response/branch handling, message/frame format, completion/exception conditions.

children.title: 4.2.26 Get Alias_ID (GAID)
children.summary: Section 4.2.26 **Get Alias_ID (GAID)** covers the `GAID` ELS used by the Alias Server to request a unique `Alias_ID` for an Alias Group identified by `Alias_Token`; includes **procedure/flow**, **message/frame format**, and **response/branch handling**. Named anchors: `GAID`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `Alias_ID`, `Alias_Token`, `Alias_SP`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `FFFFF8h`, `FFFFFDh`, `GAID (30h)`, `02h`. Protocol items: `GAID Request Sequence`; `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Addressing specifies `S_ID` = Alias Server (`FFFFF8h`), `D_ID` = Fabric Controller (`FFFFFDh`). `Table 77 – LIRR LS_ACC Payload`: defines `LS_ACC` payload for `LIRR Request`/`RLIR ELSs` acceptance (`02h 00h 00h 00h`). `Table 78 – GAID Payload`: defines `GAID` payload layout, including `Alias_Token`, `Alias_SP`, `NP_List_Length`, and `NP_List (1..n)`.

children.title: 4.2.26.1 Description
children.summary: Core topic: `4.2.26.1 Description` defines `GAID` (`Get Alias_ID`) as an ELS sent to the `Fabric Controller` by the `Alias Server` to request a unique `Alias_ID` associated with the Alias Group identified by `Alias_Token`; references `FC-FS-2` and `FC-GS-5`. Named anchors present: `GAID`, `Alias_ID`, `Fabric Controller`, `Alias Server`, `Alias_Token`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `FFFFF8h`, `FFFFFDh`, `GAID (30h)`, `Alias_SP`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`, `MSB`, `LSB`. `Table 77 – LIRR LS_ACC Payload`: shows `LS_ACC` payload format, including `02h 00h 00h 00h`. `Table 78 – GAID Payload`: defines `GAID` payload fields/word layout, including `Alias_Token`, `Alias_SP`, `NP_List_Length`, and `NP_List` entries. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, procedure/flow.

children.title: 4.2.26.2 Protocol
children.summary: Defines `4.2.26.2 Protocol` for `Get Alias_ID (GAID)`: the protocol-level exchange overview for obtaining a unique `Alias_ID` associated with an `Alias Group` via `Alias_Token`. Contains procedure/flow and response/branch handling only: `a) GAID Request Sequence` and `b) LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Named anchors present: `GAID`, `Alias_ID`, `Alias Group`, `Alias_Token`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `Fabric Controller`, `Alias Server`, `GAID (30h)`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `FFFFF8h`, `FFFFFDh`. Table references in nearby protocol context: `Table 77 – LIRR LS_ACC Payload` shows `LS_ACC` payload format (`02h 00h 00h 00h`); `Table 78 – GAID Payload` defines the `GAID` request payload fields including `Alias_Token`, `Alias_SP`, `NP_List_Length`, and `NP_List`. No figures, state machine, or timer/negotiation content.

children.title: 4.2.26.3 Request Sequence
children.summary: Section 4.2.26.3 `Request Sequence` covers the `GAID` request sequence for `Get Alias_ID (GAID)`: addressing and request payload layout sent to the Fabric Controller. Core identifiers/codes: `S_ID` = Alias Server (`FFFFF8h`), `D_ID` = Fabric Controller (`FFFFFDh`), `GAID (30h)`, `Alias_Token`, `Alias_SP`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`, `MSB`, `LSB`, “Number of `NP_List` entries = n”. `Table 78 – GAID Payload` shows/defines the `GAID` payload format: Word 0 opcode `30h`, Words 1–3 `Alias_Token` (12 bytes), Word 4 `Alias_SP`, Word 23 `NP_List_Length`, Words 25..`24 + n` `NP_List` entries. Also present on this page: `Table 77 – LIRR LS_ACC Payload`, which defines the `LS_ACC` payload (`02h 00h 00h 00h`) for `LIRR`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format.

children.title: 4.2.26.4 Reply Sequence
children.summary: Core topic: `4.2.26.4 Reply Sequence` for `GAID Request` / Get Alias_ID response behavior. Content types: `response/branch handling`, `message/frame format`, `completion/exception conditions`. `LS_RJT` rejects the `GAID Request` when an `Alias_ID` is not generated; returns reason code `Unable to perform command request` with reason code explanation from `table 147`. `LS_ACC` indicates successful completion; payload format in `Table 80 – Get Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload`, showing `Word 0 = 02h 00h 00h 00h` and `Word 1 = Alias Group Identifier`; the `Alias Group Identifier` field contains the `Alias_ID` the `Nx_Port` shall recognize in addition to its native address identifier. Also referenced in adjacent context: `Table 78 – GAID Payload`, defines the `GAID` payload/request format. No figures.

children.title: 4.2.27 Fabric Activate Alias_ID (FACT)
children.summary: Core topic: `4.2.27 Fabric Activate Alias_ID (FACT)` — `FACT` ELS sent by the `Alias Server` to the `Fabric Controller` to assign the passed `Alias_ID` as an `Alias_ID` for the passed `Nx_Ports`. Nearby anchors on this page include `Alias Group Service Parameters (Alias_SP)`, `Nx_Port List Length (NP_List_Length)`, `Nx_Port List (NP_List)`, `N_Port_ID`, `Alias_ID`, `NP_List`, `GAID Request`, `LS_RJT`, `LS_ACC`, reason code `Unable to perform command request`, `Alias Group Identifier`, and hex values `30h`, `02h`, `00h`. `Table 78 – GAID Payload`: defines the `GAID` payload/message format. `Table 80 – Get Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload`: defines successful `LS_ACC` reply payload carrying `Alias Group Identifier`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

children.title: 4.2.27.1 Description
children.summary: Core topic: `4.2.27.1 Description` defines `Fabric Activate Alias_ID (FACT)`—the `FACT ELS` sent to the `Fabric Controller` by the `Alias Server` to cause it to assign the passed `Alias_ID` as an `Alias_ID` for the passed `Nx_Ports`. Nearby referenced context includes `Alias_SP`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`, `N_Port_ID`, `Alias_ID`, `LS_RJT`, `LS_ACC`, reason code `Unable to perform command request`, `Get Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload`, `02h 00h 00h 00h`, and `Alias Group Identifier`. Tables: `Table 78 – GAID Payload` shows the `GAID (30h)` payload/message format; `Table 80 – Get Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload` defines the successful `LS_ACC` payload carrying `Alias Group Identifier`. Content types: message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

children.title: 4.2.27.2 Protocol
children.summary: Covers `4.2.27.2 Protocol` for Fabric Activate Alias_ID: `FACT Request Sequence` and `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`, with follow-on `4.2.27.3 Request Sequence` and `4.2.27.4 Reply Sequence`. [procedure/flow] [message/frame format] [response/branch handling] [completion/exception conditions] Key identifiers and codes: `S_ID`=`FFFFF8h` (Alias Server), `D_ID`=`FFFFFDh` (Fabric Controller), `FACT (31h)`, `Alias_ID`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`, `N_Port_ID`, `LS_RJT`, reason code `"Unable to perform command request"`. Defines request payload semantics and addressing, including right-aligned `N_Port_ID` in `NP_List` low-order 3 bytes. `Table 81 – Fabric Activate Alias_ID Payload`: defines request payload word/bit layout for `FACT`, `Alias_ID`, `NP_List_Length`, and `NP_List`. `Table 80 – Get Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload`: shows `LS_ACC` payload layout.

children.title: 4.2.27.3 Request Sequence
children.summary: Core topic: `4.2.27.3 Request Sequence` for `FACT Request Sequence` (Fabric Activate Alias_ID), defining addressing and request payload layout for activating an `Alias_ID` for listed `Nx_Ports`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Addressing uses `S_ID` = Alias Server (`FFFFF8h`) and `D_ID` = Fabric Controller (`FFFFFDh`). Payload fields: `FACT (31h)`, `Alias_ID`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`; `NP_List_Length` gives number of `NP_List` entries, and each `N_Port_ID` in `NP_List` is right aligned with high-order byte ignored and low-order 3 bytes containing the `N_Port_ID`. References `Table 81 – Fabric Activate Alias_ID Payload` for request format. Related response note: `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`; `LS_RJT` may return reason code `Unable to perform command request` if an `Alias_ID` is not activated. `Table 80 – Get Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload`: shows `LS_ACC` payload format.

children.title: 4.2.27.4 Reply Sequence
children.summary: Section `4.2.27.4 Reply Sequence` covers FACT reply handling for Fabric Activate Alias_ID, specifically `LS_RJT` behavior after a `FACT Request`. Core rule: `LS_RJT` signifies rejection of the `FACT Request`; an `LS_RJT` with reason code `"Unable to perform command request"` shall be returned if an `Alias_ID` is not activated as an Alias_ID. Related named anchors present in context: `FACT`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `FFFFF8h`, `FFFFFDh`, `Alias_ID`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`, `N_Port_ID`, `Table 81 – Fabric Activate Alias_ID Payload` (request payload format/fields). Table present: `Table 80 – Get Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload` — shows `LS_ACC` payload layout. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions, message/frame format, procedure/flow.

children.title: 4.2.28 Fabric Deactivate Alias_ID (FDACT)
children.summary: Core topic: `Fabric Deactivate Alias_ID (FDACT)` ELS used by the `Alias Server` to ask the `Fabric Controller` to deassign an `Alias_ID` as an Alias Group identifier for specified `Nx_Ports`; when no `Nx_Ports` remain active for that `Alias_ID`, the Fabric Controller frees it for reassignment. [procedure/flow] `4.2.28.2 Protocol`: `FDACT Request Sequence`; `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. [message/frame format] `4.2.28.3 Request Sequence`: `S_ID` = `FFFFF8h`, `D_ID` = `FFFFFDh`; payload fields `FDACT (32h)`, `Alias_ID`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`, `NP_List (1)` … `NP_List (n)`. [response/branch handling] success via `LS_ACC`, reject via `LS_RJT`. [completion/exception conditions] deactivation completion/freeing condition stated. Tables: `Table 82 – Fabric Activate Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload` shows successful `LS_ACC` payload (`02h 00h 00h 00h`); `Table 83 – Fabric Deactivate Alias_ID Payload` defines the `FDACT` request payload layout.

children.title: 4.2.28.1 Description
children.summary: Section 4.2.28.1/related text covers `FDACT` (Fabric Deactivate Alias_ID) ELS purpose and adjacent reply/request formats: Alias Server sends `FDACT` to Fabric Controller to deassign an `Alias_ID` as an Alias Group identifier for specified `Nx_Ports`; when no `Nx_Ports` remain active, Fabric Controller frees the `Alias_ID` for reassignment. Includes response/branch handling: `LS_ACC` or `LS_RJT` reply sequence; completion condition: successful completion indicated by `LS_ACC`. Exact identifiers/codes: `S_ID`=`FFFFF8h`, `D_ID`=`FFFFFDh`, `FDACT (32h)`, `02h`, `Alias_ID`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`. Message/frame format present. Table 82 – Fabric Activate Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload: shows `LS_ACC` payload format (`02h 00h 00h 00h`). Table 83 – Fabric Deactivate Alias_ID Payload: defines request payload layout and `NP_List` entries. Procedure/flow present via request/reply sequence names.

children.title: 4.2.28.2 Protocol
children.summary: Section `4.2.28.2 Protocol` indexes the `Fabric Deactivate Alias_ID (FDACT)` exchange at a protocol-outline level: `a) FDACT Request Sequence` and `b) LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Core topic: protocol steps for sending `FDACT` from `Alias Server` to `Fabric Controller` to deactivate an `Alias_ID` for specified `Nx_Ports`. Named anchors present in surrounding section context: `S_ID`, `D_ID`, `FFFFF8h`, `FFFFFDh`, `FDACT (32h)`, `Alias_ID`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`. Table `83 – Fabric Deactivate Alias_ID Payload` shows the request payload/message format, including `Word 0` opcode `FDACT (32h)` and fields `Alias_ID`, `NP_List_Length`, `NP_List`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling.

children.title: 4.2.28.3 Request Sequence
children.summary: 4.2.28.3 Request Sequence: FDACT request framing for Fabric Deactivate Alias_ID, including addressing and Payload contents sent by the Alias Server to the Fabric Controller. Addressing uses `S_ID` = `FFFFF8h` (Alias Server) and `D_ID` = `FFFFFDh` (Fabric Controller). Payload fields: `FDACT (32h)`, `Alias_ID`, `NP_List_Length`, and `NP_List` entries (`NP_List (1)` ... `NP_List (n)`), where `Alias_ID` is the Alias Group ID to deactivate and `NP_List_Length` gives the number of `NP_List` entries. Table 83 – Fabric Deactivate Alias_ID Payload: defines the FDACT Payload word/bit layout. Table 80 – Get Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload: shows the `LS_ACC` Payload format (`02h 00h 00h 00h`). Also references `LS_ACC` / `LS_RJT` reply sequence and `Table 82 – Fabric Activate Alias_ID LS_ACC Payload`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling.

children.title: 4.2.28.4 Reply Sequence
children.summary: Section `4.2.28.4 Reply Sequence` covers `FDACT` reply behavior and the transition into `4.2.29 N_Port Activate Alias_ID (NACT)`. children.title: 4.2.30.1 Description
children.summary: Covers `4.2.30.1 Description` for `N_Port Deactivate Alias_ID (NDACT)`: the `NDACT ELS` is sent by `Alias Server` to an `Nx_Port` to deactivate the passed `Alias_ID` as an `Alias_ID`. children.title: 4.2.30.3 Request Sequence
children.summary: Core topic: `4.2.30.3 Request Sequence` for `N_Port Deactivate Alias_ID (NDACT)` request delivery to an `Nx_Port`. Field detail: `Alias Group Identifier (Alias_ID)` shall contain the `Alias_ID` the `Nx_Port` no longer recognizes. Completion/response linkage in section context: `Nx_Port` shall transmit `LS_ACC` to notify deactivation; `LS_RJT` signifies rejection if the `Alias_ID` is unable to be deactivated. `Table 87` defines NDACT payload format.

children.title: 4.2.31 Discover N_Port/Service Parameters (PDISC)
children.summary: Covers `4.2.31 Discover N_Port/Service Parameters (PDISC)`: purpose and protocol for transferring Service Parameters from initiating `Nx_Port` to the `Nx_Port` at `D_ID` without affecting the operating environment, open `Sequences`, or open `Exchanges`. children.title: 4.2.31.1 Description
children.summary: Covers `4.2.31.1 Description` for `Discover N_Port/Service Parameters (PDISC)`: the `PDISC ELS` transfers `Service Parameters` from the initiating `Nx_Port` to the `Nx_Port` associated with `D_ID` without affecting the operating environment, enabling exchange without terminating open `Sequences` or open `Exchanges`. children.title: 4.2.31.2 Protocol

children.title: 4.2.31.3 Request Sequence
children.summary: 4.2.31.3 Request Sequence — PDISC request semantics and payload equivalence. Core topic: `PDISC` `Request Sequence` uses Payloads in `PDISC` and `LS_ACC` that are, except for `ELS_Command`, identical to `PLOGI` (see `4.2.7`) and corresponding `LS_ACC` (see `4.3.2`). `Service Parameters` exchanged in `PDISC` shall not modify the operating environment or Service Parameters between the two ports; `Service Parameters` contained in `PDISC` shall be ignored and no error condition reported. Contains: `message/frame format`, `response/branch handling`, `completion/exception conditions`. Named anchors: `PDISC`, `LS_ACC`, `ELS_Command`, `PLOGI`, `Service Parameters`. Table `87 – N_Port Deactivate Alias_ID Payload`: defines `NDACT (34h)` payload layout/fields (`31 .. 24`, `23 .. 16`, `15 .. 08`, `07 .. 00`).

children.title: 4.2.31.4 Reply Sequence
children.summary: 4.2.31.4 Reply Sequence covers `PDISC` reply handling and exception/completion conditions for `LS_ACC`/`LS_RJT`. Rule: no response is sent to a `PDISC` if `LR` or loop initialization occurs before `LS_ACC` is sent; an `LS_ACC` for a `PDISC` is ignored if `LR` or loop initialization occurred between the `PDISC` and the `LS_ACC`. Nearby named anchors on this page include `4.2.31 Discover N_Port/Service Parameters (PDISC)`, `4.2.31.3 Request Sequence`, `PLOGI`, `ELS_Command`, and `4.2.32 Discover F_Port Service Parameters (FDISC)`. Table `87 – N_Port Deactivate Alias_ID Payload` defines the `NDACT (34h)` payload word/byte layout. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions, message/frame format.

Named anchors present: `FDISC`, `ELS`, `Service Parameters`, `Nx_Port`, `Fx_Port`, `F_Port_ID`, `FFFFFEh`, `N_Port_IDs`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`. children.title: 4.2.32.2 Protocol
children.summary: Section `4.2.32.2 Protocol` covers the `FDISC` ELS protocol exchange for `Discover F_Port Service Parameters`: `a) FDISC Sequence` and `b) LS_ACC, LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Core topic is the protocol-level sequence and reply choices for `FDISC` at well-known `F_Port_ID` `FFFFFEh`, tied to assignment of additional `N_Port_IDs` without changing service parameters. Contains: procedure/flow, response/branch handling. Exact anchors present in surrounding section text tied to this protocol context: `FDISC`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `Fx_Port`, `Nx_Port`, `F_Port_ID`, `FFFFFEh`, `N_Port_IDs`. children.title: 4.2.32.3 Request Sequence
children.summary: Covers `FDISC` request behavior and its immediate branching into reply handling context for additional Fabric login. Core rules: `Payload` in `FDISC` and corresponding `LS_ACC` matches `FLOGI`/`LS_ACC` except `ELS_Command`; behavior depends on `S_ID` and `N_Port_ID`. If `S_ID` = zero, `FDISC` transfers `N_Port Name` and `node name` to request assignment/login of an additional `N_Port_ID`; sender must already have one `N_Port_ID` logged in, and `Service Parameters` shall not be modified. If `S_ID` is a previously-assigned `N_Port_ID`, `FDISC` carries matching `N_Port_Name` and `node name`; `Service Parameters` are ignored and no error reported. Response/branch handling and completion/exception conditions include `D_ID`, `F_RJT`, reason code `Login required`, class-specific handling for `Class 1`, `Class 2`, `Class 3`. No tables. No figures.

children.title: 4.2.32.4 Reply Sequence
children.summary: FDISC `Reply Sequence` response/branch handling and completion/exception conditions: defines `LS_ACC`/`F_RJT` behavior based on `S_ID` in the `FDISC ELS`. If `S_ID` is zero, `D_ID` in `LS_ACC` is the additional `N_Port_ID` being assigned, and the Fabric considers `Fabric Login` complete for that additional `N_Port_ID`. If `S_ID` is not zero and is currently logged in, `D_ID` of `LS_ACC` is set to the `S_ID` of the `FDISC ELS`. Error branch: if `S_ID` = zero and no `N_Port ID` is logged in, or `S_ID` is a non-zero `N_Port_ID` not currently logged, return `F_RJT` with reason code `Login required` to the `S_ID` for `Class 1`/`Class 2`; discard for `Class 3`. No tables. No figures.

No message/frame format, state machine, timer/negotiation, or completion/exception conditions are specified in this section text. Tables: none. Figures: none.

children.title: 4.2.34 Third Party Process Logout (TPRLO)
children.summary: Core topic: `4.2.34 Third Party Process Logout (TPRLO)` defines the `TPRLO` ELS purpose and basic protocol for invalidating operating environments at a recipient `Nx_Port` for a specified `TYPE`, equivalent to prior `Process Login` participants issuing `PRLO`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions. Protocol anchors: `TPRLO Request Sequence`; `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`; Request Sequence addressing with `S_ID` = initiating `Nx_Port`, `D_ID` = destination `Nx_Port`. Related carryover table: `Table 91 – Response summary to FDISC/PDISC` shows reply behavior for `FDISC`, `PDISC`, `ADISC` by responding port/status/class, including `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `F_RJT`, `LOGO`, `Discard`, states `Logged in`/`Not Logged in`, and reason codes `Unable to perform command request`, `N_Port Login required`, `Login required`, `Command not supported`, `Request notsupported`.

children.title: 4.2.34.1 Description
children.summary: Section `4.2.34.1 Description` defines `Third Party Process Logout (TPRLO)`: the `TPRLO ELS` invalidates operating environments at the recipient `Nx_Port` for the specified `TYPE`, removing image pairs and associated resources, and has the same effect as if all `Nx_Ports` that performed `Process Login` for that `TYPE` had performed `PRLO` with the recipient `Nx_Port`. Nearby protocol/context anchors included on this page: `4.2.34.2 Protocol` (`TPRLO Request Sequence`, `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`) and `4.2.34.3 Request Sequence` with `S_ID` and `D_ID` field roles for initiating and destination `Nx_Port`. `Table 91 – Response summary to FDISC/PDISC`: response matrix for `FDISC`, `PDISC`, `ADISC` by `Logged in`/`Not Logged in`, `Responding Nx_Port`, `Responding F_Port Controller`, and `LS_ACC`/`LS_RJT`/`F_RJT`/`Discard` outcomes. Content types: response/branch handling, completion/exception conditions.

children.title: 4.2.34.2 Protocol
children.summary: TPRLO protocol overview. Section `4.2.34.2 Protocol` defines the `Third Party Process Logout (TPRLO)` exchange at a high level: `a) TPRLO Request Sequence` and `b) LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Core topic is the request/reply protocol for `TPRLO`, with adjacent context that `S_ID` designates the initiating `Nx_Port` and `D_ID` designates the destination `Nx_Port`. Includes nearby response handling reference from `Table 91 – Response summary to FDISC/PDISC`, which shows outcomes for `FDISC`, `PDISC`, `ADISC` by `Responding Port Status` (`Logged in`, `Not Logged in`) and responder type/class (`Responding Nx_Port`, `Responding F_Port Controller`, `Class 1, 2, or 3`, `Class 1, or 2`, `Class 3`), including `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `F_RJT`, `LOGO`. Content types: procedure/flow, response/branch handling.

children.title: 4.2.34.3 Request Sequence
children.summary: Section `4.2.34.3 Request Sequence` covers the `TPRLO` ELS request sequence: `S_ID` designates the Nx_Port initiating `TPRLO`, `D_ID` designates the destination Nx_Port. Includes [procedure/flow], [message/frame format], [response/branch handling], [completion/exception conditions]. `Table 91 – Response summary to FDISC/PDISC` shows responses for `FDISC`, `PDISC`, `ADISC` by responding port status (`Logged in`, `Not Logged in`) and responder type/class, including `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `F_RJT`, `LOGO`, `Discard`, reason codes `"Unable to perform command request"`, `"N_Port Login required"`, `"Command not supported"`, `"Request not supported"`, `"Login required"`. `Table 92 – TPRLO Payload` defines payload words/fields: `TPRLO (24h)`, `Obsolete (10h)`, `Payload Length` (shall be `20`), `Logout Parameter page`. `Table 93 – TPRLO logout parameter page` defines `TYPE Code or Common Service Parameters`, `TYPE Code Extension`, `Global Process Logout`, obsolete validity/associator/N_Port_ID fields, and reserved bits.

children.title: 4.2.35 Clock Synchronization Request (CSR)
children.summary: Section `4.2.35 Clock Synchronization Request (CSR)` covers the `CSR ELS` used to request the `Clock Synchronization Server` to either send or quit sending periodic `Clock Synchronization Update (CSU) ELS frames` or `Clock Synchronization primitives`, depending on the implemented method (`see FC-FS-2`). Named anchors present: `4.2.35.1 Description`, `4.2.35.2 Protocol`, `Clock Synchronization Request Sequence`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Content types: procedure/flow (request and reply sequences), response/branch handling (`LS_ACC` or `LS_RJT`), completion/exception conditions (accept vs reject reply). Table present in section material: `Table 94 – TPRLO LS_ACC Payload` — defines `TPRLO LS_ACC Payload` fields including `02h`, `Obsolete (10h)`, `Payload Length`, and `Logout Parameter page` (carryover table adjacent to CSR text).

children.title: 4.2.35.1 Description
children.summary: Section `4.2.35.1 Description` defines the purpose of the `Clock Synchronization Request (CSR)` ELS. Core topic: `CSR` is used to request the `Clock Synchronization Server` to either send or quit sending periodic `Clock Synchronization Update (CSU)` ELS frames or `Clock Synchronization primitives`, depending on the method implemented (`FC-FS-2`). Contains: message/frame purpose, timer/negotiation context via periodic synchronization behavior. Nearby named protocol anchors introduced with this section family: `4.2.35.2 Protocol`, `Clock Synchronization Request Sequence`, `LS_ACC`, `LS_RJT`, `LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. Also present on the page is `Table 94 – TPRLO LS_ACC Payload`, which shows the `TPRLO LS_ACC Payload` format, including `Payload Length`, `Logout Parameter page`, `02h`, obsolete `10h`, and value `20` (contextual adjacent table).

children.title: 4.2.35.2 Protocol
children.summary: Section `4.2.35.2 Protocol` covers `Clock Synchronization Request (CSR)` protocol handling. Content is minimal and contains a `procedure/flow` plus `response/branch handling`: `a) Clock Synchronization Request Sequence`; `b) LS_ACC or LS_RJT Reply Sequence`. This section indicates the exchange pattern for the `CSR ELS`, specifically request sequencing and acceptance/reject reply sequencing, but provides no frame fields, timers, states, or exceptions beyond the named reply alternatives `LS_ACC` and `LS_RJT`. Nearby carried-over reference on the same page: `Table 94 – TPRLO LS_ACC Payload` — shows the `TPRLO LS_ACC Payload` format/definition, including `02h`, obsolete `10h`, `Payload Length`, and `Logout Parameter page`; not a CSR table. No figures.

children.title: 4.2.35.3 Request Sequence
children.summary: Section `4.2.35.3 Request Sequence` defines the `CSR` request for Clock Synchronization updates: addressing uses `S_ID` as requesting source `Nx_Port`, and `D_ID` as `Clock Synchronization Server (FFFFF6h)` or `Fabric Controller (FFFFFDh)`. Contains `procedure/flow`, `message/frame format`, and `response/branch handling`. Payload is `Table 95 – CSR Payload`, showing `Word 0` `CSR (68h)` and fields `Clock Sync Mode`, `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_M`, `CS_Implemented_L`, `CS_Update_Period`. `Table 96 – CSR Clock Sync Mode Meaning` defines `Clock Sync Mode` values `00h`, `01h`, `02h–FEh`, `FFh`, with different meanings when sent to the `Clock Sync Server` versus the `Fabric Controller` (enable, return requested/unspecified Quality of Service parameters, disable, reserved). Also defines `CS_Accuracy_Mantissa` and `CS_Accuracy_Exponent` bit fields and the `T_reference ± ...` accuracy expression.

children.title: 4.2.35.4 Reply Sequence
children.summary: Core topic: `4.2.35.4 Reply Sequence` for Clock Synchronization Request/Reply handling of `CSR`. Response/branch handling: `LS_RJT` = rejection of the `CSR` command; `LS_ACC` = server agrees to perform the action requested in the `CSR Payload`. Message/frame format: `LS_ACC Payload` includes `Clock Sync Mode`, `CS_Accuracy`, `CS_Implemented_MSB`, `CS_Implemented_LSB`, `CS_Update_Period`; `Clock Sync Mode` in the `CSR LS_ACC Payload` shall contain the value received in the `CSR Payload`. `Table 97 – CSR LS_ACC Payload`: shows `LS_ACC` payload word/bit layout, including `Word 0 = 02h 00h 00h 00h`. `Table 98 – CSU Clock Sync Mode Meaning`: defines `00h` = enabled, `01h - FEh` = reserved, `FFh` = disabled. Also defines `CS_Accuracy_Mantissa`, `CS_Accuracy_Exponent`, `T_reference`, and semantic meanings of implemented MSB/LSB and update period.

children.title: 4.2.36 Clock Synchronization Update (CSU)
children.summary: Section `4.2.36 Clock Synchronization Update (CSU)` defines the `CSU` ELS used by the Clock Synchronization Server to send its current clock value to clients (`FC-FS-2`). Covers `4.2.36.1 Description`, `4.2.36.2 Protocol`, and `4.2.36.3 Request Sequence`. Protocol elements include `Clock Synchronization Update Request Sequence` and `No Reply Sequence` (response/branch handling). Request/frame details: `Information Category` in `Header word 0, bits 24-27` = `Solicited Data (0001b)`; `S_ID` = Clock Synchronization Server well-known address `FFFFF6h`; `D_ID` = receiving `Nx_Port(s)`; Payload contains `CSU (69h)` and `Clock Count` (8 bytes) (message/frame format). `Table 99 – CSU Payload`: defines payload word/bit layout. `Table 100 – Clock Count Field Meaning`: defines bytes `0`–`7`, from `MSB` to `LSB`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling.

children.title: 4.2.36.1 Description
children.summary: Clock Synchronization Update (CSU) ELS description: used by the Clock Synchronization Server to send its current clock value to clients (see FC-FS-2). Contains message/frame format and response/branch handling. Protocol notes: Clock Synchronization Update Request Sequence; No Reply Sequence; Request Sequence. Header/identifier details called out here: Information Category (Header word 0, bits 24-27) = Solicited Data (0001b); S_ID = Clock Synchronization Server well-known address (FFFFF6h); D_ID = Nx_Port(s). Payload includes `CSU (69h)`, `Reserved`, and `Clock Count` (8 bytes). Table 99 – CSU Payload: shows payload word/bit layout. Table 100 – Clock Count Field Meaning: defines byte 0 (MSB) through byte 7 (LSB) of `Clock Count`. Also notes Bit 7 in Word 2 = 1/106,25MHz.

children.title: 4.2.36.2 Protocol
children.summary: Section `4.2.36.2 Protocol` covers the `Clock Synchronization Update (CSU)` ELS protocol behavior for the Clock Synchronization Server: `Clock Synchronization Update Request Sequence` with `No Reply Sequence` noted, plus request details in `4.2.36.3 Request Sequence`. Content types: procedure/flow, message/frame format, response/branch handling. Key identifiers/fields/codes: `Information Category` = `Solicited Data (0001b)` in `Header word 0, bits 24-27`; `S_ID` = Clock Synchronization Server well-known address `FFFFF6h`; `D_ID` = destination `Nx_Port(s)`; Payload opcode `CSU (69h)`; `Clock Count` (8 bytes); `Bit 7 in Word 2`. `Table 99 – CSU Payload` defines the CSU payload word/bit layout. `Table 100 – Clock Count Field Meaning` defines byte-by-byte meaning of the `Clock Count` field from byte `0 (MSB)` to byte `7 (LSB)`.
